newsSince: No material company-specific news since June 9. The micro-LED 'slow-and-wide' AI optical-interconnect program advanced to product development in March 2026 (predates window, ~2030 revenue), and CES 2026 optical-sensing/microLED launches are older. Stock +6.2% since June 9 (+115% YTD) appears flow/recovery-driven, not news-driven.

sources.label: ams OSRAM microLED AI optical interconnect (LEDinside)

verdicts.rankRationale: Latent sensors/photonics lens: the AI-photonics development agreement (datacenter optics) modestly supports the edge-AI/datacom thesis, but it was disclosed at Q1 (May 7), not within this window. Turnaround on track, expectations still low. Slight positive tilt, rank holds; +6.2% is sentiment flow.
verdicts.bull: AI-photonics development deal opens a datacenter-optics path; restructuring delivering ahead of plan toward FCF in 2027.
verdicts.neutral: Optical-sensing recovery and AI optics are early; no fresh catalyst since June 9.
verdicts.bear: Balance-sheet scars and consumer/auto cycle still overhang the $2.4B name.

verdicts.rankRationale: CPO lens: still not a direct co-packaged-optics cable/engine winner. The micro-LED interconnect program is the only CPO-relevant angle and it is unchanged since June 9 (prototype-stage, ~2030 revenue). Keeps it at the bottom of the list; no re-rank.
verdicts.bull: Micro-emitter interconnect IP could become CPO-relevant if 'slow-and-wide' optics gain traction.
verdicts.neutral: Optical expertise present but no direct CPO product/revenue today.
verdicts.bear: Indirect, distant, and competitively contested CPO exposure; balance-sheet overhang.
verdicts.primaryRisk: No near-term CPO monetization; financing/cycle risk
